title: Strong year for public markets bolsters CIRI’s private equity investments
summary: CIRI Chief Financial Officer Stig Colberg. Photo by Chris Arend. In April, CIRI shareholders will receive their 2013 CIRI Annual Report, which will detail the results of CIRI’s business operations and investments. From real estate to tourism to marine services, shareholders will learn how each sector performed. While high-profile projects like Fire Island Wind and the new Fireweed Business Center may generate the most p...

title: CIRI prepares for shareholder meetings
summary: CIRI is preparing for its shareholder information meetings and annual meeting. This year, the events begin in Anchorage where CIRI welcomes shareholders on the evening of April 18 to an open house at the CIRI Building. The event will allow shareholders to meet with the Board of Directors. Along with...

title: A word from the president, March 2014
summary: By: Sophie Minich, CIRI president and chief executive officer The unusually warm weather that settled over Anchorage this month has many of us thinking of spring—a time of renewal and growth. CIRI has done a lot of growing over the past year, and soon shareholders will have a chance to learn about the...

title: CIRI distributed resource revenue payments on April 2
summary: CIRI distributed resource revenue payments to its at-large shareholders in the amount of $13.8833 per share, or $1,388.33 to at-large shareholders who own 100 shares, on Tuesday, April 2. Section 7(i) of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA) requires each regional corporation to share 70 p...

title: Spotlight: Megan Saunders
summary: Courtesy of Megan Saunders. As an AmeriCorps volunteer just out of college, CIRI descendant Megan Saunders was certain of one thing: “I wanted to have this big, broad impact on the world. I was working with the International Rescue Committee, a refugee resettlement agency. It was exactly what I thought I wanted to do.” But as she soon discovered, “It was a lot of time in front of a computer.” Meanwhile...

title: Birth announcement, Caden Saunders
summary: Caden Saunders. Courtesy of Megan Saunders. CIRI descendant Megan Saunders and husband Stewart Saunders announce the birth of their son Caden William Saunders. Caden was born July 9, 2013. title: CIRI launches redesigned CIRI.com
summary: In an effort to improve the look and functionality of CIRI’s online presence and to better highlight shareholder services, CIRI unveiled a newly revamped website at ciri.com . summary: CIRI shareholders who are online have the option of enrolling in CIRI’s eNewsletter Program to receive interactive versions of the Raven’s Circle in lieu of paper newsletters. eNewsletters contain links and other features not available in the paper newsletter. summary: The winner of the February 2014 $200 Stock Will Participation Prize is Cora E. Simmonds. title: Chance encounter leads to book publication 27 years later
summary: When CIRI shareholder Janna Yancey, of Anchorage, was a little girl, she dreamed of two things: seeing her name on a card in a library catalogue and meeting the pope. “I was always really drawn to the Italian culture,” Janna said—an affinity that’s led to the publication of her first coauthored book...

summary: Erin Haldane. Courtesy of Corbin Haldane. Erin Haldane, daughter of CIRI shareholder Corbin Haldane, was selected for an internship at the Georgia O’Keeffe Museum in Santa Fe, New Mexico. title: Missing shareholders, March 2014
summary: The following CIRI shareholders do not have a current mailing address on record. When CIRI mail is returned as undeliverable, the CIRI records are placed on hold and any distributions are held pending receipt of a valid address. title: In memory, March 2014
summary: Leona Kay Geffe, 49 Leona Kay Geffe passed away Dec. 29, 2013. Ms. Geffe was born March 28, 1964, in Noorvik, Alaska. She was known by her friends and family as always smiling, giving, caring and compassionate. title: CIRI protects its land and resource rights at Kenai Loop
summary: CIRI staff have been actively protecting CIRI’s land and resource rights at the Kenai Loop gas field since the summer of 2013 against trespass and uncompensated gas production by Buccaneer Alaska, LLC (Buccaneer), the operator of the field. The Kenai Loop field was discovered after having drilled the Kenai Loop 1-1 discovery well in A...

title: Applications available for Coronado Park Townhome rentals
summary: Cook Inlet Housing Authority’s (CIHA) construction of the much anticipated Coronado Park rental townhomes is scheduled to be completed mid-May 2014.
